content:
# Session 27 (iter-027) — Review Summary

## Metadata
- Prover lanes: **1** (opus) — FBC `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean`. SNAP = plan-phase only
  (defeq probe refuting Option-A + blueprint rewire), NO prover lane.
- Sorry count (firsthand cold build, EXIT=0): **FBC 5** (cocycle@2017, glue@2041, 3 dead-mate
  @2432/2707/2747), **SNAP 6** (green monolith, untouched). **Net frontier elimination: 0.
  New sorry-free decls: 0.** One new decl `ring_square_cocycle` (skeleton, sorry).
- **iter-026 RED regression FIXED** — cold `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FlatBaseChange`
  = EXIT=0, 0 errors (verified firsthand). blueprint-doctor: 0 findings. sync_leanok iter-27 sha
  b065ada, +80 \leanok (all on Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex — the deterministic reaction to
  restoring the file from RED to GREEN).

## FBC — eqToIso boundary-bridge cocycle FOLD decisively REFUTED (the iter-027 result)
- **Step (a) green restore (done):** deleted the dead commented `ring_square_glue_natTrans` block
  (~L1950–2009), the temporary `ring_square_cocycle_probe`, and the iter-026 bombing `exact …_probe`
  fold; reverted glue body to typed `sorry`. iter-026 RED gone.
- **Step (b) the fold (refuted), four attempts:**
  - 4-boundary bridge (e2/e3/e4/e6; shared X1/X5 ends unbridged): `have key` cold-build bombs at the
    `qincl` arg — `(kernel) deterministic timeout at isDefEq` @L2249 (shared X1 start boundary
    `g.source ≟ qincl.source` forces cross-unification).
  - 6-boundary bridge (split X1/X5 too, e1..e6): `key` builds PAST `qincl` (all six heavy isos
    elaborate) but bombs at the trailing six inline `rfl`s @L2258. ⇒ **ACCUMULATION** (6 boundary
    defeqs + 6 heavy iso elaborations > per-declaration 200000-hb budget), not one pathological
    boundary. Splitting `rfl`s into separate `have`s does not help (budget is per-declaration);
    `maxHeartbeats 1e6` forbidden.
  - fvar / pre-proven-boundary-lemma branch (own budget per boundary): `key` would build cheaply BUT
    the bridges cannot be collapsed — LSP-verified (`lean_run_code`): `simp only [eqToIso_refl,
    Iso.refl_trans]` is a NO-OP on an fvar boundary `e2 : objA = objB` (objA ≢_syn objB), since
    `eqToIso_refl` fires only on a syntactic `X = X`. No Mathlib lemma collapses
    `eqToIso (h : objA = objB)` for objA ≠ objB syntactically.
- **Net (decisive):** the eqToIso bridge RELOCATES the compounded boundary defeq (between
  cocycle-application and the final match) but cannot ELIMINATE it. Inline-`rfl` ⇒ collapsible but
  build-compounds >200k; fvar ⇒ cheap build but un-collapsible. **Both branches dead — DO NOT
  re-attempt the eqToIso boundary-bridge fold in either form.** This comprehensively blocks the
  fold family (carrier-bearing iter-024, carrier-free iter-026, eqToIso-bridge iter-027).
- **iter-028 escalation (handed off):** `CategoryTheory.CatCommSq.hComp` — register each leg as a
  `CatCommSq` instance, paste via `hComp`/`vComp`, extract `.app M` ONCE at the end via
  `congrArg (·.hom.app M)`; composition adjacency is the shared FUNCTOR matched structurally by
  `Functor.comp` congruence (no heavy OBJECT defeq forced). See `analogies/fbc-fold-adjacency.md`.

## SNAP — untouched (green monolith); route pivoted in plan phase
- No prover this iter. The plan phase's cheap move-free defeq probe DECISIVELY killed Option-A
  (`⊗_loc`'s tensor object is NOT defeq to `sheafification.obj (F♭⊗G♭)` — kernel TYPE mismatch, not
  a timeout) and pivoted to the section-element `Γ(η)`-pushforward route (`TensorPower.mul_assoc`
  idiom); blueprint `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex` rewired. SNAP prover lane = iter-028 (gated on
  the mandatory blueprint-reviewer + blueprint-clean of the rewritten chapter). Associator wall
  `tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs_native` UNTOUCHED, now confirmed OFF the critical path.

## Subagent findings (reports in `.archon/logs/iter-027/`)
- **lean-vs-blueprint-checker fbc027** (1 must-fix / 1 major / 1 minor; pins clean):
  - **must-fix:** `ring_square_cocycle` Lean signature (6 bare isos + 6 `eqToIso` bridges, no
    adjunctions) does NOT match `lem:ring_square_cocycle` (mate-calculus, hypotheses (i)–(iv)). After
    `subst` it reduces to an arbitrary-iso equality that is false-in-general, sorry-patched — not the
    blueprint theorem. → Addressed by me with a `% NOTE (iter-027 review)` flagging the mismatch +
    refutation; full reconciliation is a blueprint-writer/prover job for the planner.
  - **major:** both `lem:ring_square_cocycle` and `…mate_glue` proof prose still advertise the
    REFUTED eqToIso fold as "realized/working", contradicting the Lean. → Addressed with `% NOTE`
    markers at both blocks.
  - **minor:** stale commented Lean citation of nonexistent `lem:ring_square_glue_natTrans` (inside a
    dead/commented block).
  - **clean:** cocycle `\lean{}` pin correct (→ `ring_square_cocycle`, no `…_probe`); no proof-level
    `\leanok` over-marked (both `\leanok` are statement-level only, accurate for formalized-with-sorry).
- **lean-auditor iter027** (2 must-fix / 3 major / 1 minor; no excuse-comments, no axioms):
  - **must-fix ×2:** no-op `set_option maxHeartbeats` guards in `FlatBaseChange.lean` — L2374 (before
    the `/-! ### Seam 2 … -/` doc header) and L2409–2412 (before `/-! ### Seam 3 … -/`). In Lean 4
    `set_option X in cmd` consumes the next command; a `/-! … -/` doc comment IS a standalone command,
    so the option never reaches the theorems. `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` (L2432) and the Seam-2
    decls run UNGUARDED → will break the cold build once the gating sorries are closed. (These sit in
    the dead-mate apparatus slated for excision.) → recommendations.md.
  - **major:** archaeology block L1950–2009 (dead `ring_square_glue_natTrans`); ~220-line proof-journal
    above the single `sorry` in `…mate_glue` (L2041–2264); **visibility asymmetry** — 5 decls
    (`MonoidalPresheaf`, `pshModMonoidal`, `pshModBraided`, `pshModSymmetric`, `Wsheaf`) are `private`
    in `SectionGradedRing.lean` but PUBLIC in `SectionGradedRingLocalized.lean`, same namespace →
    latent duplicate-declaration kernel error if any future module imports both. → recommendations.md.
  - **minor:** triple-redundant stacked `set_option` (L2409–2411).

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`, `lem:ring_square_cocycle`: added `% NOTE (iter-027 review)` —
  the "realized route" prose is REFUTED (eqToIso fold failed two cold builds); the formalized Lean
  signature drops hypotheses (i)–(iv) and is false-as-written/sorry-patched (statement mismatch);
  planner to dispatch a blueprint-writer.
- `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`, `lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_mate_glue` proof:
  added `% NOTE (iter-027 review)` — the "realized fold" prose is refuted in-file (glue body is a
  typed `sorry`); state OPEN, fed by the open cocycle; iter-028 pivots to `CatCommSq.hComp`.
- No `\leanok` override needed (sync's statement-level markers verified accurate by lvbc-fbc027).

## Coverage debt
`archon dag-query unmatched` = **84** `lean_aux` nodes (mostly SNAP `Modules.*` + ~15 FBC helpers),
all pre-existing. No materially new debt this iter (`ring_square_cocycle` replaced the deleted
`…_probe`; it carries a `lem:ring_square_cocycle` blueprint entry). Listed for the planner in
recommendations.md.

## Key findings
- The FBC ring-square glue fold family is now refuted on all three sub-routes (carrier-bearing,
  carrier-free, eqToIso-bridge). The kernel obstacle is invariant: any abstract-cocycle FOLD must
  unify its conclusion against a goal whose telescope adjacencies are defeq-but-not-syntactic, and
  that unification (or its post-collapse) always forces the compounded boundary whnf. The structural
  escape must avoid the OBJECT-level `≪≫` telescope entirely — hence the `CatCommSq.hComp`
  functor-level paste (matched by `Functor.comp` congruence) for iter-028.
